title: Issues, Reviews, Pull Requests, and Team Workflow · History Defense
summary: Move a change from issue to review to integration with traceable decisions, bounded branch scope, and respectful collaboration. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a team contribution and review protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.
Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.
Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Issue 5 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.

content.text: Issue 5 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Integration Policy symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: gitworkflows Manual — Controls integration, topic branch, merge, release, and distributed maintainer workflow guidance.

hints: Name the Integration Policy precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Issue 5 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Passing CI alone grants authority to merge a change.

stimulus.title: Integration Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Passing CI alone grants authority to merge a change.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Workflow Policy evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Workflow Policy assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Workflow Policy has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Workflow Policy precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Integration Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.

hints: Name the Integration Policy precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Passing CI alone grants authority to merge a change.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
